meta.name: DASS-21 Depression Anxiety Stress Scales
meta.description: The DASS-21 is a 21-item self-report scale that simultaneously assesses the severity of three negative emotional dimensions: depression, anxiety, and stress. It is the short form of the DASS-42, widely used in clinical screening and research.
meta.author: Lovibond & Lovibond (1995)
meta.reference: Lovibond SH, Lovibond PF. Manual for the Depression Anxiety Stress Scales. 2nd ed. Sydney: Psychology Foundation; 1995.
meta.instruction: Please read each statement and select the option that best describes how much the statement applied to you over the past week.

commonChoices.label: Did not apply to me at all
Applied to me to some degree
Applied to me a considerable degree

questions.text: I found it hard to wind down
I was aware of dryness of my mouth
I couldn't seem to experience any positive feeling at all
I experienced breathing difficulty (e.g., excessively rapid breathing, breathlessness in the absence of physical exertion)
I found it difficult to work up the initiative to do things
I tended to over-react to situations
I experienced trembling (e.g., in the hands)
I felt that I was using a lot of nervous energy
I was worried about situations in which I might panic and make a fool of myself
I felt that I had nothing to look forward to
I found myself getting agitated
I found it difficult to relax
I felt down-hearted and blue
I was intolerant of anything that kept me from getting on with what I was doing
I felt I was close to panic
I was unable to become enthusiastic about anything
I felt I wasn't worth much as a person
I felt that I was rather touchy
I was aware of the action of my heart in the absence of physical exertion (e.g., sense of heart rate increase, heart missing a beat)
I felt scared without any good reason
I felt that life was meaningless

ranges.label: Normal Range
ranges.description: Your combined depression, anxiety, and stress scores are within the normal range. This indicates that your mood has been relatively stable recently, with no significant signs of persistent sadness or loss of interest.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good mental health
Keep up healthy habits and coping strategies
Maintain regular social activities and hobbies
Set aside time each day for activities you enjoy
Practice gratitude by noting positive moments in your life

ranges.label: Mild Distress
ranges.description: Your combined score indicates some level of emotional distress. These mild symptoms may occasionally affect your energy and motivation, but they typically do not interfere significantly with daily functioning.
suggestions: Review subscale scores to identify the primary source of distress
Learn stress management and emotion regulation techniques
Try 30 minutes of aerobic exercise daily, such as walking or cycling
Maintain a regular sleep schedule and avoid staying up late
Practice mindfulness meditation or deep breathing exercises

ranges.label: Moderate to Severe Distress
ranges.description: Your combined score is elevated. Consider reviewing individual subscale scores and seeking professional help. At this level, feelings of low mood may cause persistent fatigue and reduced interest in activities, potentially affecting daily life and relationships.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Review subscale scores to identify areas needing the most attention
Consider consulting a mental health counselor for professional guidance
Try self-help exercises based on cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT)
Wake up at a fixed time each day to establish daily structure

depression.label: Normal
depression.description: Depression subscale score is within the normal range. This indicates that your mood has been relatively stable recently, with no significant signs of persistent sadness or loss of interest.
suggestions: Maintain regular social activities and hobbies
Set aside time each day for activities you enjoy
Practice gratitude by noting positive moments in your life
Keep a consistent sleep schedule and exercise routine
Stay connected with friends and family

depression.label: Mild Depression
depression.description: Mild depressive symptoms present. Monitor your mood changes. These mild symptoms may occasionally affect your energy and motivation, but they typically do not interfere significantly with daily functioning.
suggestions: Try 30 minutes of aerobic exercise daily, such as walking or cycling
Maintain a regular sleep schedule and avoid staying up late
Practice mindfulness meditation or deep breathing exercises
Stay in touch with trusted friends or family members
Reduce time spent alone and engage in social activities

depression.label: Moderate Depression
depression.description: Notable depressive symptoms. Consider seeking professional help. At this level, feelings of low mood may cause persistent fatigue and reduced interest in activities, potentially affecting daily life and relationships.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health counselor for professional guidance
Try self-help exercises based on cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT)
Wake up at a fixed time each day to establish daily structure
Set small, achievable daily goals to gradually restore activity levels
Reduce alcohol intake and avoid unhealthy coping strategies

depression.label: Severe Depression
depression.description: Severe depressive symptoms. Strongly recommended to seek medical attention. Your score suggests notable emotional distress that may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Reaching out to a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule an appointment with a mental health professional as soon as possible
You can call a mental health helpline for immediate support
Make sure you have someone you trust nearby for companionship and support
Follow professional recommendations and cooperate with your treatment plan
Maintain basic daily routines and self-care activities

depression.label: Extremely Severe Depression
depression.description: Extremely severe depressive symptoms. Please seek professional help immediately. Your score suggests notable emotional distress that may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Reaching out to a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule an appointment with a mental health professional as soon as possible
You can call a mental health helpline for immediate support
Make sure you have someone you trust nearby for companionship and support
Follow professional recommendations and cooperate with your treatment plan
Maintain basic daily routines and self-care activities

anxiety.label: Normal
anxiety.description: Anxiety subscale score is within the normal range. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine
Cultivate mindful awareness habits
Maintain strong social connections

anxiety.label: Mild Anxiety
anxiety.description: Mild anxiety symptoms present. Consider practicing relaxation techniques. Mild tension or worry is common in everyday life. Simple self-regulation techniques can often help manage these feelings.
suggestions: Learn and practice progressive muscle relaxation
Try 5-10 minutes of mindfulness meditation daily
Reduce caffeine and stimulant intake
Record situations that trigger anxiety and try to analyze them rationally
Exercise regularly, at least three times a week

anxiety.label: Moderate Anxiety
anxiety.description: Notable anxiety symptoms. Consider seeking professional help. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety
Practice relaxation techniques daily and maintain regular exercise
Identify and reduce avoidance behaviors; gradually face anxiety-provoking situations
Maintain regular sleep and exercise habits

anxiety.label: Severe Anxiety
anxiety.description: Severe anxiety symptoms. Strongly recommended to seek medical attention. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system

anxiety.label: Extremely Severe Anxiety
anxiety.description: Extremely severe anxiety symptoms. Please seek professional help immediately. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system

stress.label: Normal
stress.description: Stress subscale score is within the normal range. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance
Cultivate hobbies and interests that bring you joy
Nurture healthy social relationships

stress.label: Mild Stress
stress.description: Mild stress symptoms present. Consider learning stress management techniques. Mild stress is a normal part of life. Learning stress management techniques can help you handle it more effectively.
suggestions: Identify your main sources of stress and try to address them gradually
Learn time management and prioritization skills
Set aside 15-30 minutes daily for relaxation activities
Maintain moderate exercise and healthy eating habits
Practice saying no and setting reasonable boundaries

stress.label: Moderate Stress
stress.description: Notable stress symptoms. Consider adjusting your pace of life and seeking support. At this level, stress may cause persistent tension, mood fluctuations, and reduced work efficiency or rest quality.
suggestions: Consider discussing stress management strategies with a counselor
Evaluate and adjust major stressors in your work or personal life
Build daily habits of relaxation and self-care
Consider reducing unnecessary commitments and responsibilities
Ensure adequate sleep time

stress.label: Severe Stress
stress.description: Severe stress symptoms. Strongly recommended to seek professional support. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments
Ensure basic needs for sleep, nutrition, and rest are met
Consider temporarily reducing workload or seeking help to share responsibilities

stress.label: Extremely Severe Stress
stress.description: Extremely severe stress symptoms. Please seek professional help immediately. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments
Ensure basic needs for sleep, nutrition, and rest are met
Consider temporarily reducing workload or seeking help to share responsibilities
